items.caption:
I really thought the dry skin + that lingering chlorine smell after pool days was just something we had to deal with 😅

But my daughter was dealing with pretty intense irritation and I couldn’t just sit around and let her suffer.

Turns out, chlorine actually binds to the skin and hair… so just rinsing or washing it isn’t always enough to fully remove it.

Once I learned that, I added this one simple step right after swimming and it honestly changed our whole routine.

Now I always use a chlorine-removing spray before bath time and it’s made such a difference in their skin (and no more that pool smell sticking around 🙃).

If you’re doing lots of pool days this summer, this is definitely something I wish I had known sooner.

Comment SPRAY and I’ll send you the link to my very own after swim spray: Chlorine Cleanse 🫶🏼

And share this with another mom who needs to add this to her pool bag!!

#momlife #toddlermom #swimming #summer #babyswim

My discount code will make it the same price as during prime day 🥳

items.transcript: This past year I bought a steam cleaner, a carpet cleaner, a spot cleaner, and a window cleaner, and they all sit in my closet unused. There's actually like only one cleaning device that I actually use every single day, and that is my floor cleaner. This guy right here is the TINCO S6, which is one of their more affordable options, but it can still do so much. My two-year-old decided to leave half of his breakfast on the ground, so we're going to clean that up first. I love that this lays completely flat so I can get under furniture, including the high chair. My daughter was playing with sand, so we're going to clean that up. It still blows my mind that this thing can pick up something like sand, but then also spills like water and coffee. I'm going to demonstrate the anti-tangle feature with some bright colored hair so you can see that it actually doesn't get stuck. And then probably the most impressive feature is the self-cleaning, which cleans all of the internal components. And then I just grab this, and this is all I have to clean.

items.caption:
One of the biggest lessons I learned as a mom was that chlorine doesn’t just rinse off the skin. 🫠

After pool days, my daughter used to deal with red, dry, itchy skin constantly — and I had no idea chlorine could continue sitting on the skin and causing irritation even after swimming.

Now I always use a chlorine-removing spray before bath time and it’s made such a difference in their skin (and no more that pool smell sticking around 🙃).

If you’re doing lots of pool days this summer, this is definitely something I wish I had known sooner.

How much did your birth cost you?? Where did you deliver? 

#birth #homebirth #pregnant #motherhood #positivebirth
items.transcript: I remember when I was considering having a home birth, I was trying to figure out how much it was going to cost me. So I'm going to share exactly that, all of the nitty gritty numbers and what it cost us to have our baby at home. Obviously, it's going to depend on the provider, but mine had a $5,000 package that included everything. It included the prenatal, all in-office visits. It included our labor and delivery, obviously in my home. She came to the home. And then it included postnatal visits as well. This included a 24-hour wellness visit where my midwife came to our home, as well as a four-day visit where she came to the home. And then we did a two-week visit. She also was like super helpful. My son was jaundiced and so we would jump on the call and I would text her and we would send pictures. And she was so helpful during that postpartum period. And then we also went in office for a six-week visit. So that was all included in the $5,000, which we did have to pay out of pocket. So when we signed the contract, we had to pay $1,000. Then at 20 weeks, we paid $1,000. At 28 weeks, we paid $1,000. And then at 36 weeks, we paid the remainder, which was $2,000. All of that was put into one claim that we sent to my insurance. I have Blue Cross Blue Shield. And they reimbursed based on my coverage, which they paid me $1,600 back reimbursed. So it ended up being about $3,400 that we paid for the entirety of everything that we needed to give birth.

items.caption:
Did you know chlorine doesn’t just stay in the water - it bonds to your skin and floats in the air you breathe? 😷

When chlorine mixes with things like sweat, sunscreen, hair, or lotion, it forms harmful byproducts (like HAAs) that can irritate skin, trigger rashes, and affect your lungs (especially for little ones!!)

Every time we got out of the pool my daughter would itch nonstop and her skin was red and irritated.

That’s why I created Chlorine Cleanse — a vitamin C–based spray that instantly neutralizes chlorine on contact.

✨ Safe for babies, toddlers & sensitive skin
✨ Helps prevent rashes, dryness & that lingering “pool smell”
✨ Non-toxic, fragrance-free, and made in small batches
✨ Great before + after swimming or even public showers

Use it before swimming or showering in public places, and again after to rinse off chlorine (you can even reapply during sunscreen touch-ups!).

Comment SPRAY and I’ll DM you the link 💌

#nontoxicliving #momhacks #chlorine #summer #momlife

We did this with both kids and I promise it’s so much easier to teach a tiny 6M old instead of a crazy toddler 😆

#babyled #babyledweaning #crunchymom #blw #momlife

items.caption:
Nobody hands you a manual when you become a parent.

One day you’re changing diapers and picking out cute baby clothes… and the next you’re trying to understand ingredient labels, water quality reports, air filters, plastics, artificial fragrances, food dyes, and things you never even knew existed before having kids.

It can feel overwhelming. 

Imagine a world where we didn’t have to spend all of our time researching these things and could focus on the good ol’ ABC’s. 

I’m curious, what was the first thing you “researched” as a parent? What led you to start? What did you learn? 

#relatablemom #crunchymom #newmomlife #commonsourcepartner #motherhood

Watching this back reminds me how incredible giving birth is.

Unmedicated birth isn’t about being tough.
It’s about understanding what’s happening, trusting your body, and training your mind.

Birth is like a marathon.
If you train for it, you experience it differently.

Comment BIRTH for my free guide to help you prepare and feel your most confident 🫶

#birthstory #birth #positivebirth #homebirth #unmedicatedbirth

I clearly should have done more research, so learn from my mistakes! 😅

I’m using the new pump from Eufy and if you’re in the market I cannot recommend it enough- I couldn’t believe the output I was getting! 

#pumping #breatfeeding #newmom #momlessons #relatablemotherhood

Yep, I still breastfeed my 24 month old.. And I’ll continue until he’s ready to stop. 

Some days it’s once..
Some days it’s a few times.
Sometimes it’s comfort, sometimes it’s connection, sometimes it’s just part of our routine.

Extended breastfeeding isn’t all or nothing—it’s just what works for you and your baby in this season.

And one thing that’s helped me keep going (without feeling drained or overwhelmed) is having tools that actually support me… especially on the days I need a little more flexibility.

I’ve been using the eufy Wearable Breast Pump S2 Pro, and I didn’t expect to love it as much as I do. It doesn’t feel like that harsh, surface-level buzzing I’ve experienced before—it actually feels like a deeper massage, which has made a huge difference for comfort and output.

The heat + massage combo has been especially helpful for letdown and just making pumping feel less like a chore and more like a quick, easy reset during the day. And the fact that it’s wearable (and I can literally move around with it) makes it so much more realistic to fit into life with two kids.

I also love that I can quickly check alignment without guessing (especially at night with the built in nipple light 🙃), which sounds small but makes a big difference.

For me, this season isn’t about doing things perfectly—it’s about finding what makes this journey feel sustainable.

Whether you breastfeed for a few weeks, a year, or beyond… there’s no one right way. Just what works for you 🤍

If you’re in a similar season and need something more flexible, you can use my code to save! Comment EUFY & I’ll send it right to you 🖤

#breastpump #wearablebreastpump #eufybreastpump #eufybreastpumps2pro #extendedbreastfeeding
@eufybaby

Most shoes are designed for looks… not development.

Things like:
• narrow toe boxes
• stiff, thick soles
• heavy materials
• even small heels (!!)

All of that can actually affect how your toddler walks, balances, and develops over time.

Once I learned what to look for, it completely changed how I shop for shoes—and I’ll never go back to just buying the “cute” ones 🙃

If you’re shopping for summer sandals, just keep it simple:
👉 wide toe box
👉 flexible sole
👉 lightweight
👉 no heel

That alone puts you ahead of most options out there.

Comment “SPLASH” and I’ll send you my favorite toddler sandal options that actually check all the boxes 👇

And follow along if you want more:
non-toxic swaps • toddler essentials • barefoot shoes • and all the things I wish I knew as a first-time mom 💛

#toddlershoes #momlife #crunchymom #momhack #momtips @tenlittlekids

As a mom of two, babyproofing has honestly been less about making my house look “perfect” and more about creating spaces where my kids can safely explore, climb, play, and just be kids without me feeling on edge 24/7 😅

These are the babyproofing products we actually use daily and the ones that made the biggest difference for us — especially during the curious toddler stage when they somehow find danger in EVERYTHING 🫠

I tried to focus on products that are:
• practical + easy to use
• don’t completely ruin the look of your home
• actually hold up over time
• make life easier instead of more stressful

Because the reality is… you can’t watch them every second. Having a few solid systems in place gives so much peace of mind 🤍

#babyproof #babyproofing #momhack #toddlermom #preparingforbaby

One thing I wasn’t prepared for before giving birth was how much time there actually is to relax between contractions.

For some reason, I imagined labor as one long, nonstop wave of pain from the first contraction until baby arrived… but that wasn’t my experience at all. Most contractions only lasted about a minute, and then I’d get a full minute (sometimes longer) where everything completely eased up again.

Those breaks gave me time to breathe, reset, laugh, talk, move around, and remind myself: okay… I can do another one. 🤍

I think seeing birth portrayed as constant panic and intensity made it feel so much scarier than it actually was for me. The pauses in between mattered so much more than I ever realized.

What surprised you most about birth? Let me know in the comments! 👇

If you’re preparing for birth and want to learn everything I wish I would’ve known - comment BIRTH and I’ll send you my free guide. 🖤

#positivebirth #homebirth #birthstory #unmedicatedbirth #pregnancy

Comment SHOES & I’ll send you the to all my favorite barefoot toddler shoes (including these from ten little) 

Most shoes marketed towards kids are nowhere near wide enough. And if your child has wide feet you wanna make sure that you’re buying shoes specifically designed for them. 

Some features I personally look for in shoes 👇

✅ a flexible sole so they can feel the ground and get that sensory feedback while building all of those small muscles used to support their entire body.

✅ a wide toe back so their toes can spread out which helps their balance as well as proper development 

✅ a flat sole because heels create unnecessary pressure on the ball of their feet and mess with the alignment of their entire body. 

✅ breathable to prevent friction which causes some serious discomfort

Barefoot | barefoot shoes | foot friendly | toddler shoes | baby shoes | mom tips #momtips #babyshoes #toddlershoes #momsofinstagram #motherhood @tenlittlekids

This was one of the coolest parts of labor for me- especially watching it back and realizing how instinctual everything was. 

In the middle of labor, I suddenly had this overwhelming urge to get down on my hands and knees. No one told me to do it- my body just knew. 

As soon as I was on my hands and knees, I realized how uncomfortable the bathroom floor was so I stood up, walked to the birthing tub and proceeded to start pushing. 

Shortly after, I was holding my baby in my arms. 

If you let it, birth has a way of letting your body just take over. 

If you’re preparing for birth and want to feel more informed and empowered, I put together a free birth guide with things I wish I knew before labor, postpartum prep, mindset tips, and more. 

Comment BIRTH & I’ll send it to you 🖤 (be sure your following or you won’t see the message!) 

#positivebirth #birthpreparation #birthplan #unmedicatedbirth #homebirth
